reviews.review_msg: NOT a good instructor for this course! Could not learn anything from him! Waste of money and time
A very nice prof, choose him without any doubts.
Too nice

Nice prof. BUS 100 was a joke anyway so having a caring guy lecture for an hour about how we should "chase our dreams" was honestly very enjoyable. At the very least, he was inspiring and was there to help with any questions.

He was my first business professor, and thus was my first impression of Beedie. He was more than just a lecturer; he was a figure of guidance. My friends and I felt a sense of belonging and pride for Beedie, thanks to him. Taught us material and taught us engagement alike. Great personality.

Stephen is very experienced and does not read off lecture slides. He adds on using examples. You don't really need the textbook, slides should be sufficient to do well in this course.   Only downside is you don't really know what to expect for exams and marking scheme for the exam is unclear.

Half way through the semester and I realized that Spector is not the guy I should have taken this course with. For someone who emphasizes that accounting is all about practice, but DOES NOT provide the solutions for assignment problems after the assignments have been graded, I feel he has lost touch of what it is like to be a student.

Excellent professor. He is technical but also encourages his students to think outside the box. He cares about his students and is very responsive to requests for help. Best accounting professor I have had.

He's a very good prof and tries to explain everything clearly. What I liked most about his class is that he doesn't give exams based on dead theory/memory-based questions. Instead, he gives you cases and you are expected to answer accordingly. Very useful if you plan on taking CASB. One of the best in SFU!

He is a nice guy at heart. However, the main issue is that he subconsciously stereotypes students and assigns grades accordingly. What I am saying is, it is impossible to get a high mark if you're Asian and quiet in the class. Try to get to know him personally, talk in class, and become one of his favorites and you will do well regardless.
Very Good

Amazing prof, seriously! He keeps you interested in the material, and is VERY helpful after class, during break, at office hours, etc. Had him now for 2 semesters and am taking another class with him next semester.

He has good personality and is willing to help you. However, he did not prepare IFRS material and is VERY disorganized. Sometimes he teaches IFRS and yet sometimes it's GAAP..extremely confusing. Does not show effort in making complex material easier to understand. I did not learn much from his 321..might have to redo.. NOT RECOMMENDED for 321
Fun teacher
Intelligent, fair, helpful, and interesting. Stephen is simply one of the best profs in the business faculty!
awesome
Stephen's a good teacher but an even better person.  His courses themselves are okay, but the guy himself is awesome.  One of my favourite instructors by semesters' end.  Also applies to 424

That's the easiest course to teach. He doesn't have to say anything or provide any lecture notes because the students will do it for him. Do you want to study by using student-version powerpoint slieds? Good luck, then. They are not reliable and vague.

Lectures could be a bit better.  Often time you hear a bunch of words and phrases all rushing into ur ears but u have no idea what he was talking about.  Anyways Nice Prof!  He likes to joke around sometimes, which makes the lectures a little less boring. Do ALL ur assignments and Redo them a few times to get good marks. Pretty Tough Exams!
not that easy.. essay questions on exams..feel like a HR course..he's a nice guy though..

Pretty nice professor.  The materials are alright, but the last part of the course can be difficult.  The exams and marking were fair as a whole.  If you are going to take 254, Spector is a decent choice.

he's nice. Always try to help students, and answers quickly especially on his email. course was annoying since it's a new course, but he's nice. anyway if you are doing accoutning major, you gotta face this course. take it with him.
No Comments
read the txtbook if u want a good mark ...lecture, u can go if u hv nth to do...dont' rely on the powerpoints and the exam prep.  the prof is nice ^^

Dont go to the lecture it is a waste of time. He is an extremely nice person though. Read the book 4 times! thats all you need. AND do questions as much as you can. The materials are easy but questions can be confusing.
I liked his style of teaching, and he's a good lecturer.  Exams really test you on general knowledge, not specific things.  Overall, it was a good class.
His exams are fair boz he said he hate tricky questions.So just study his notes and you will be fine.
Nice guy, not very useful lectures. fair exams. Overal he gets a B in teacihng. he is kinda funny too.
No Comments
He never responds to your email, unfair!Even though you got A, but you only learned a little.

He is a well-meaning teacher, he tries to make everyone get a good grade, as long as you keep up with assignments, and do practice exams questions, and online (mcgraw-hill) multiple choice questions you can get an A. I did, and i missed over half the lectures. he's pretty funny too, for an accountant
No Comments
Spector does not read letters sent to him, nor is he a fair person.  He has very poor people skills.  he also accuses people of things before the facts are in.
Lazy prof. He pretends to be nice, but the students care about the knowledge and the fairness, right? Those are what he won't give to you.
Bad, sad...even though you did good, cause you might not really learn something.

Can you imagine a prof. who teaches you nothing? He is one of these professors. Why did SFU choose a guy like him? Maybe because he is very "sociable" and "clever"? Not to take his course if you like to learn something

This is a most lazy person I have ever met. Really! Take my advice -- if you want to be a CA or CGA, and if you really want to learn something, aviod him at all cost! has millions of excuses to aviod marking your assignments. He chooses the most easy knowledge to teach and avoid the important and difficult parts! Not responsible, not nice at all!
The grading is totally subjective. He's not a hardworking guy, and personally didn't learn a lot from this course. However, stories in the class are sometimes interesting.
He is nice, but I don't feel like learning anything from him.  Just study by myself.
Unimpressive! I'd rather take this class with Anne MacDonald.
Simply da BEST! My first ever "A+"

Exams are not fair. There are too many subjective essay questions. I don't know why accounting doesn't deal with numbers in this course. Reading text is useless and u need some common sense to answer questions in the exams.
soooooooooooo goooooooooooooooooooooooooood!!!

I have no idea why so many people said he is a good prof.  I am very good at accounting and I have done well in all my accounting courses. But I did this by reading the text by myself. Spector just does not how to teach. He always skip the difficult and important thing in the lec.
A great prof....just do ur readings and do as many problem, and exercise questions in the text!!

Awesome prof. Best in SFU. People who complain about not learning anything should blame the useless BUS 319 course material not the prof. He gives you an analysis of the textbook notes, so you dont need to read text. I wish someone would post about how BUS 424 is with him coz I need to do that class
would you like some more crispy creams????????

nice and soft guy. his MTC questions are not very accurate or critical, thus requiring common sense instead of textbook standards to answer correctly. He is as good as Ann Macdannald, but have different characteristics.

He is nice, so you may feel good because your mark is high,but he is lazy and lack of responsibility. Because of high average, your grade cannot be as high as you expected. I dislike him since he marked the exam very subjectively. Learn nothing

had him last fall, but never got a chance to rate him. He is a very good prof who will do everything to make sure u understand the contents. Reasonable guy too, but the class avg was relatively higher than other bus classes.

funny lecturer, very clear. Exams questions are not straight forward, but not tricky type. Need to know the materials very well then will be fine. Don't need to read reserved readings, important things are all in the notes + during the lectures
midterms are ez, but watch out for the hard final.
Agree with last comment.  BUT he's very nice and forgiving because he actually cares about his students.

good guy, good lecture. i still hold a grudge cause one question worth 25% of the final couldn't be answered and i spent WAY too much time on that question which made my mark suffer considerably. i ended up with a C and i was going into the final wit 93%
his nice, friendly, but yet i count on textbook rather than his lecture

His lecture is not quite helpful because he follows his notes while he teach... and his notes are too simple. You cant distinctly says he's an easy or hard prof b/c he is not those tough or tricky prof. he jus gives u "surprises"

It's a tough course but Spector makes it easier.  His exams are mostly calculations.  If you like theory better take it with MacDonald.  The midterm was hard but he tries hard to make things clear in lecture.  Final was simple.

good prof, probably best prof ii business admin cause he really makes ACCT interesting, he really encourages u to learn and lots of joke in his class. U will like his lecture but not exams since it's much harder then what u would expect, recomd it!

The tests are not easy, but the whole experience was better than I thought.  Very nice, knows what he's talking about.  No note taking.  The constant jokes, although lame, add a spice of life and excitement.  He actually makes accounting -somewhat- fun.
He gives you the easiest examples but the tests are way harder!

I got an A in the course, but I thought some of the exam questions were worded improperly and unfairly marked because the question was ambiguous to begin with.  He cracks bad jokes in lecture, which makes him somewhat endearing, yet I don't recommend him.

Great prof.  Friendly, approachable, patient.  He seems to like what he does and is keen on helping students understand.  When it comes to studying, review his class notes and know the homework assignments.
This has to be the nicest professor in the Faculty of BA.  Definitely take this course with him, and make sure you do all the homework assignments.
If I could give you one advice: GO OVER HOMEWORKS BEFORE EXAMS. thats basically all you have to do. He is so nice. Best prof ever.
easy course, funny guy, great class.
He makes an "academically challenging" course comprehensive with his slades and teaching ability. Exams were a little on the difficult side but were fair. Do you homework!
Great prof!
Great Prof.. funny and nice!!! Exam was too long but not very difficult!!  Best lectures ever cause he is soooooooo funny
Great prof. you can tell he spent a lot of time on his lecture slides. exams and final was fair.
nice one
great teacher... exams were fair
No Comments
Very easy course which means that very competitive too. Our class average was 87%.
he really cares about students and teaching. Exams r not too easy, but fair.
Excellent prof.  Really cares about the students.
He was very clear and did not try to trick you purposely.  Great Prof
